user.title: Setup Blueprints Updater
user.description: Do you want to setup Blueprints Updater? It will scan your local blueprints and look for updates.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only a single instance of Blueprints Updater is allowed.

init.title: Settings
init.description: Configure the behavior and settings for Blueprints Updater.

reload.name: Reload blueprints
reload.description: Scans all local blueprints for new files and checks for updates immediately.

restore_blueprint.name: Restore Blueprint
restore_blueprint.description: Restores a blueprint from a numbered backup file (.bak.1, .bak.2, etc.) if available.

entity_id.name: Blueprint Entity
entity_id.description: The update entity of the blueprint you want to restore.

version.name: Backup Version
version.description: Which backup version to restore (1 = newest, 2 = older, etc.)

update_all.name: Update All Blueprints
update_all.description: Automatically downloads and installs all available blueprint updates simultaneously.

backup.name: Create Backup
backup.description: Create numbered backup files for all updated blueprints before overwriting.

missing_backup.message: Missing backup file. A backup may not have been created during the previous update.

invalid_version.message: Invalid backup version. Must be between 1 and the configured max backups.

common.auto_update_message:
The following blueprints have been automatically updated:

{blueprints}
common.auto_update_warning: Warning: Updates may carry backward incompatibility risks if the author introduces breaking changes. It is highly recommended to enable the backup option before installing.
common.usage_warning: Warning: This update will affect {count} running {domain}(s).
common.install_error: Cannot install blueprint: {error}. The remote file has errors and cannot be safely applied.
common.content_missing: Blueprint content is missing or could not be fetched
common.semantic_sync_notice: This blueprint is semantically identical, but requires a structural update to align with the new canonical formatting (e.g. newline normalization, metadata injection).
